title: What the viewer opens to
question: When the explorer loads, should the detail panel start empty or open a decision?
rationale: On load, open the most recently active decision (reusing d7's last-activity sort) so the panel starts on the decision most likely relevant — the one just touched. Chosen over the old empty placeholder (a dead end) and the first list item, which in phase order is the oldest.

tradeoffs.note: the panel opens on a decision, not a placeholder
surfaces what changed last — the 'what's new' question
jumps the list to that item if it's far down
makes no assumption about what you want
first thing you see is 'select a decision' — a dead end
'first' in phase order is the oldest, least-useful decision
